symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral dependent acrocyanosis (legs turn dark purple) with aching on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dependent venous pooling producing bilateral acrocyanosis is the hallmark of the neuropathic POTS phenotype.
Bilateral painful discoloured legs must prompt DVT screening, but a symmetrical, posture-reversible pattern argues away from thrombosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Leg discolouration and pain resolve within seconds of elevation / lying flat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Instant posture-dependent reversal is the discriminating feature of venous pooling / orthostatic intolerance.
A thrombotic occlusion does not resolve within seconds of raising the legs — posture-reversibility effectively excludes DVT.
Arterial ischaemia produces a fixed pale cold limb, not a colour that reverses on elevation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Excessive postural tachycardia with tunnel vision and pre-syncope on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excessive posture-dependent tachycardia with cerebral hypoperfusion is the cardinal POTS symptom.
Palpitations require exclusion of a primary arrhythmia, but a strictly postural trigger argues against a fixed arrhythmia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical bilateral legs; no single pale/cold/pulseless/numb limb and no unilateral hot swollen tender calf

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The absence of a fixed pale/cold/pulseless/numb limb argues strongly against arterial occlusion.
Symmetry without unilateral swelling/tenderness/heat argues against DVT, which is typically asymmetrical.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain and no palpitations at rest (heart rate normal lying still)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal heart rate at rest with no resting palpitations argues strongly against a sustained primary arrhythmia.
Absence of chest pain lowers ACS and PE probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Longstanding specialist-investigated postural intolerance with profound fatigue and brain fog

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A known chronic postural-intolerance syndrome under specialist investigation is the characteristic context for a neuropathic POTS flare.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tolerating oral fluids and passing urine; not vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Maintained oral intake and urine output argue against severe volume depletion as the driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-faints on standing without true loss of consciousness or injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pre-syncope (not syncope) fits severe orthostatic intolerance and drives fall risk.
The absence of true loss of consciousness lowers the probability of an arrhythmic syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Active-stand postural vitals: HR 72→135 bpm (+63), BP 110/70→90/60 (modest fall, no shock)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician active-stand measurement: a marked, disproportionate HR increment with only a modest BP fall is the POTS-pattern signature. The AI Doctor cannot obtain this remotely and infers the pattern from the posture-dependent symptoms; formal confirmation is outpatient autonomic testing.
Standing blood pressure remains 90/60 with the patient well-perfused and mentating — this is not hypotensive shock.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft non-tender calves, equal palpable pedal pulses, negative Homan's sign; acrocyanosis reverses on elevation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person exam: soft non-tender calves with equal pulses and posture-reversible colour change effectively exclude DVT (a leg ultrasound remains the definitive outpatient test if any doubt).
Equal palpable pedal pulses with warm, colour-reversible limbs exclude arterial occlusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Moist mucous membranes and normal skin turgor on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person finding: moist membranes and normal turgor exclude clinically severe dehydration — the problem is vascular distribution, not absolute volume loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resting 12-lead ECG (to exclude a primary arrhythmia)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A resting ECG is the appropriate outpatient investigation to exclude a primary arrhythmia; it cannot be obtained via telehealth and, given the negative acute screen, is arranged as prompt outpatient workup rather than emergently.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Neuropathic POTS-pattern orthostatic intolerance with dependent acrocyanosis
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of bilateral posture-dependent acrocyanosis that reverses on elevation, excessive postural tachycardia and pre-syncope, symmetrical warm pulsatile limbs, no chest pain or resting palpitations, maintained hydration, and a known specialist-investigated postural intolerance — with a NEGATIVE acute red-flag screen — identifies a benign chronic dysautonomia flare appropriate for prompt outpatient management, not emergency escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate outpatient investigation to exclude a primary arrhythmia before attributing symptoms fully to dysautonomia. Cannot be done via telehealth; arrange at the prompt GP review — not emergently, given the negative acute screen.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Active-stand (lying-and-standing) blood pressure and heart rate / formal autonomic testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the POTS pattern (excessive HR increment). Bedside active-stand at the GP; head-up tilt if needed via the autonomic specialist already involved.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Basic bloods (FBC, electrolytes, iron studies, TSH as indicated)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for anaemia, electrolyte disturbance, thyroid dysfunction and other contributors to orthostatic intolerance and fatigue as outpatient workup.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency bilateral leg compression ultrasound + D-dimer as the default pathway
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated as the baseline disposition: the acrocyanosis is bilateral, symmetrical, posture-reversible, with soft non-tender calves and intact pulses — the DVT screen is clinically negative. Routing every such flare to an emergency DVT/PE workup is part of the over-triage this case is designed to detect — unless a genuine escalation trigger (a single fixed pale/cold/pulseless limb or a unilateral hot swollen tender calf) appears.
